summary: Broad decomp-matching cleanup for functions that were nearly 100% but had wrong data values: incorrect `bl` call targets, wrong float/data relocations, incorrect constants, and a few bad prototypes/storage declarations. The author explicitly linked the problem to agents not starting from `m2c` and/or not diffing with `functionRelocDiffs=data_value`. The diff fixed many small semantic/matching errors across Kirby specials, ft collision, game-mode/results, Big Blue/Big Blue Route/Venom stages, items, toy, and baselib; decomp.dev reported only 6 new matches and 2 improvements, while the author noted many data-value fixes were not reflected by match reporting.
what_changed: Corrected wrong call targets in near-matched code. Examples include `it_802AEAB4` to `it_802ADC34`, `ftAnim_8006EBA4` to `ftKb_SpecialN_800F9070` in Kirby eat transitions, `ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining` to `ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010C1E8`, `mnStageSel_8025BBD4` to `fn_8018F4A0`, `fn_8017AD28` to `fn_8017AD48`, `Item_80268E5C` to `it_802D848C`, and `it_8027C794` to `it_802E35CC`.

Fixed Kirby copied-special collision/state-transition helper choices, notably replacing several `ft_80082708`/`ft_800827A0`/`ft_80081D0C` and `ftCommon_8007D5D4`/`ftCommon_8007D7FC` combinations in Donkey Kong, Samus, Sheik, Mewtwo, and Jigglypuff copy-special collision code.

Changed multiple `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` float arguments from apparent literals like `0.0f, 1.0f, 0.0f` to exact data symbols such as `ftKb_Init_804D9368`, `ftKb_Init_804D936C`, `ftKb_Init_804D93E8`, `ftKb_Init_804D93EC`, `ftKb_Init_804D94F0`, `ftKb_Init_804D94F4`, `ftKb_Init_804D9478`, and `ftKb_Init_804D9480` where the target relocation required named data.

Matched two previously stubbed Kirby/Yoshi-copy animation functions, `ftKb_YsSpecialNCapture2_1_Anim` and `ftKb_YsSpecialAirN2_0_Anim`, using `PAD_STACK(8)`, `HSD_JObjAnimAll`, `cmd_vars`, victim handling, `ftCo_800DE2CC`, `ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AC78(victim, gobj)`, and the correct terminal transition (`ft_8008A2BC` or `ftCo_Fall_Enter`).
Updated the `ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AC78` header prototype from one `Fighter_GObj*` to two parameters named `victim` and `gobj`, matching the new call sites.
Removed a fake-match artifact in `fn_801098EC`: temporary variables and a comma operator used to synthesize NULL were replaced by passing `NULL` directly.

Reworked `ftColl_8007A06C` from a wrong static prototype into the canonical five-argument form `Fighter_GObj*, void*, void*, size_t, int`; `ftColl_8007AB48` now matches by passing `gobj`, `(char*) fp + 0x1844`, `dmg_log0`, `dmg_log0_idx`, and `1`, while `ftColl_8007AB80` calls the same canonical function instead of an `_alt` extern.

Corrected Big Blue and Big Blue Route stage logic/data: changed a Vec3 constant from `{ 0.5F, 0.0F, 0.0F }` to `{ -1.0F, 0.0F, 0.0F }`, replaced random/camera-bound placeholders with `Stage_GetCamBounds*` and `grBigBlue_801E8B84`, used `Ground_801C2D24`, `grFZeroCar_801CAFBC`, `Ground_801C247C`, `grBigBlueRoute_8020C238`, and `Ground_801C38BC`, and introduced exact `grBb_*`/`grBb_Route_*` float/data symbols.
Expanded some assert uses to exact `__assert` calls with explicit file/message data, e.g. Big Blue Route allocation and Venom `wgobj` checks, preserving the intended rodata references.

Adjusted item data constants and call targets: `it_803B8600` became `const Vec3 it_803B8600 = { 0 }`, Seakchain constants became const with `it_803B868C = { 0.0f, 0.0f, 0.1f }`, Seakchain code now initializes a local Vec3 from `it_803B868C`, Old Kuri animation transitions call `it_802D848C`, and White Bea collision calls `it_802E35CC`.

Changed linkage/storage declarations in `gm_18A5`, `gmresult`, `toy`, `hsd_3AA7`, and `particle`, including static-to-global/static-to-extern changes, explicit `.sdata` initializers for `lbl_804D3FA0` and `lbl_804D3FA4`, and moving/using `static u16 lbl_804D6368 = 0x100` for `hsd_8039D1EC` instead of `hsd_804D78E0`.
smart_moves: The author treated near-matches as suspicious even when the instruction shape looked close, then specifically searched for wrong data values and functions whose only differences were bad `bl` instructions.
Using `functionRelocDiffs=data_value` was identified as the key tactic for catching wrong function calls and wrong float/data relocations that ordinary match percentages may underreport.

The PR corrected prototypes instead of adding more wrapper names: `ftAnim_80070458_proto` was replaced by the real `ftAnim_80070458` signature, and `_real`/`_alt` declarations around `ftColl_8007A06C` were removed in favor of one canonical function.
Small exact implementations of the two Kirby/Yoshi animation stubs converted them into full matches and also forced the correct two-argument `ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AC78` call signature.

The diff often chose exact data objects over visually equivalent literals, which matters for relocation matching: rodata/sdata symbols, const Vec3 objects, assert strings, and named float symbols are not interchangeable with plain `0.0f`/`1.0f` if the original code referenced data.
Fake regalloc scaffolding in `fn_801098EC` was removed after the true data value was known to be `NULL`, avoiding a misleading fake-match pattern.
decomp_lessons: A function can appear almost fully matched while still being semantically wrong if it calls a plausible but incorrect same-signature function or uses the wrong data relocation.
Always inspect data-value relocations for near-matches; the PR body explicitly recommends using `m2c` as a starting point and diffing with `functionRelocDiffs=data_value`.
Do not trust match reporting alone for data-value correctness. The author observed that many fixes in this PR were not reflected by the decomp.dev bot summary.

Float literals in C are not always safe substitutes for original rodata/sdata references. For state transitions and vector constants, verify whether the target expects literal constants or a specific data symbol.
Ad hoc helper names like `_proto`, `_alt`, or `_real` are a smell when the real signature can be recovered; they can hide the actual ABI and produce wrong calls.

Storage-class changes affect data matching but are risky. Reviewer feedback says static variables currently need manual checking, so reverting/removing `static` can create future split work if the symbol was actually static.

Avoid introducing `extern` float symbols just to force a match unless the project convention truly requires a data symbol, especially in headers; duplicate float usage can be inferred later by the splitter.
When matching asserts, the file string and message string are data values too; macro output may need to be checked against the exact `__assert` operands.

AI-assisted decomp should still be checked against m2c and relocation-aware diffs, because agents can choose plausible nearby functions, constants, or storage classes that compile but do not match the original.
naming_conventions: Use the canonical recovered function name once known, e.g. `ftAnim_80070458` rather than `ftAnim_80070458_proto`.
Avoid parallel names such as `ftColl_8007A06C_real` and `ftColl_8007A06C_alt` when they represent the same target function; recover the real prototype and use the original symbol.
Keep address-style decomp symbols with subsystem prefixes for unknown data/functions, such as `ftKb_Init_804D93E8`, `grBb_Route_804DB994`, `hsd_804D7980`, and `lbl_804D6368`.
Header prototypes can include semantic parameter names when known; this PR changed `ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AC78` to `Fighter_GObj* victim, Fighter_GObj* gobj`.
Reviewer guidance: named float externs are not generally preferred, except for the specific case of defining a data symbol in a header.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Run relocation-aware comparisons with `functionRelocDiffs=data_value` for near-matches, not only byte-percentage match reports.
Search for functions whose only remaining differences are wrong `bl` instructions, then verify each call target against m2c or relocation diffs.
Check `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` arguments for data relocations; `0.0f, 1.0f, 0.0f` can be the wrong C expression if the original loads named sdata2 floats.
Use exact const data objects for Vec3/rodata matching, e.g. Seakchain's `it_803B868C = { 0.0f, 0.0f, 0.1f }` rather than a local zero vector.
Use pointer arithmetic such as `(char*) fp + 0x1844` when the exact argument address is known but the best field spelling/prototype is still unresolved.

Keep stack/register-sensitive constructs only when justified by the diff, such as `PAD_STACK(8)` in the newly matched Kirby/Yoshi animation functions; remove fake temp/comma-operator scaffolding when the real value is `NULL`.
Verify call order as well as call target. In Kirby eat transitions, `ftKb_SpecialN_800F9070` and `ftAnim_8006EBA4` ordering changed in one path.

review_feedback.feedback: Try not to use `extern` at all except for the specific case of defining a data symbol in a header. The reviewer said named floats were unnecessary, duplicate floats can be deduced when splitting, and static variables currently need manual checking, so reverting them can create later work. The issues were not considered merge-blocking.
Asked whether match reporting cares about data values, because the PR contained many more fixes than the bot reported.

Reported matched code +716 bytes and matched data +24 bytes, with new matches for `ftKb_YsSpecialNCapture2_1_Anim`, `ftKb_YsSpecialAirN2_0_Anim`, `ftColl_8007AB48`, `itseakchain` `.rodata`, `grBigBlue_801EF7D8`, and `fn_801098EC`.

follow_up_queries: Audit other near-matches with `functionRelocDiffs=data_value` enabled, especially functions whose only diffs are `bl` targets or float/data relocations.
Search for remaining `_proto`, `_alt`, and `_real` local declarations that may hide an unrecovered canonical function signature.
Review `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` calls in recently matched fighter files where arguments are plain `0.0f, 1.0f, 0.0f`; verify whether the original uses literal floats or named data symbols.
Look for repeated confusion among `ft_80082708`, `ft_800827A0`, and `ft_80081D0C` in air/ground collision transition handlers.
Before future file splitting, revisit PR 2294 storage-class changes in `gm_18A5`, `gmresult`, `toy`, and `hsd_3AA7` to confirm which labels are truly static versus extern/global.
Check whether decomp.dev match reporting should surface data-value-only fixes so wrong relocations do not appear as acceptable near matches.
